segments.paradiso-14-g64: 'E sì come' is a conventional simile starter meaning 'and just as.' 'al salir' uses the infinitive as a noun ('the rising'), with the prepositional contraction 'al' = a + il. 'di prima sera' = 'of early evening' (prima here = 'first/early'). 'comincian' is 3rd person plural present ('they begin') and agrees with the following plural subject 'nove parvenze.' 'per lo ciel' = 'through/over the sky' (per used like 'across/in'), and 'nove parvenze' = 'nine apparitions/appearances.'
segments.paradiso-14-g65: 'sì che' = 'so that.' 'la vista' ('the sight' or 'the eye') is the grammatical subject. 'pare' is 3rd singular present of parere ('seems'). The construction repeats with 'e non par vera' where 'par' is a poetic/contracted form of 'pare' and 'vera' = 'true/real' — together: 'the sight seems, and yet seems not real.'
segments.paradiso-14-g66: 'parvemi' = parve + mi, an impersonal/personal perfect-like expression meaning 'it seemed to me' (literary). 'lì' = 'there.' 'novelle sussistenze' = 'novelle' (new) modifying 'sussistenze' (existences/ beings / manifestations) — medieval vocabulary; these are the items whose action follows. 'cominciare a vedere' is an infinitival clause governed by 'parvemi': 'it seemed to me that new existences began to be seen.'
segments.paradiso-14-g67: This continues the infinitival coordination: 'e fare un giro' = 'and to make a circuit/turn.' 'un giro' = 'a circuit/turn'; 'di fuor da' (archaic ordering) = 'outside of.' 'l’altre due circunferenze' = 'the other two circumferences/circles' (feminine plural agreement on 'altre' with 'circunferenze'). The implied subject of 'fare' is the 'novelle sussistenze' just mentioned.
segments.paradiso-14-g68: 'Oh' is an exclamation. 'vero' intensifies as 'true' or 'veritable.' 'sfavillar' is an infinitive used as a noun here meaning 'sparkling' or 'scintillation' (poetic noun use of a verb). 'del' = di + il 'of the.' 'Santo Spiro' = 'Holy Spirit' or literally 'Holy Breath' (Spiro is an older/poetic form related to 'spirito'/'spiritus'). The whole is an exclamatory invocation of a divine glittering.
segments.paradiso-14-g69: 'come' = 'as' or 'how.' 'si fece' is reflexive/passive-like perfect (fece = passato remoto of fare) meaning 'it became' or 'it made itself' — here 'it became.' 'sùbito' = 'suddenly' (adverb) and 'candente' = 'glowing, burning.' 'a li occhi miei' = 'to my eyes' (prepositional dative), and 'che' introduces the relative clause referring back to 'occhi.'
segments.paradiso-14-g70: 'vinti' is a past participle ('defeated'/'overcome') agreeing with 'occhi' (eyes): 'which, overcome...' — used predicatively to describe the eyes' state.
segments.paradiso-14-g71: 'nol' = non + lo (object clitic) = 'not it' (i.e. 'did not bear/endure it'). 'soffriro' here is an archaic past form meaning 'they did not suffer/they could not endure' — 3rd person plural past sense matching 'occhi' (they = the eyes). Together: 'which, overcome, did not endure it.'
segments.paradiso-14-g72: 'Ma' = 'but.' 'Bëatrice' is the proper name. 'sì' = 'so' (intensifier) and 'bella e ridente' = 'beautiful and smiling' (two coordinating adjectives). 'mi si mostrò' is a reflexive/clitic construction meaning 'she showed herself to me' or idiomatically 'she appeared to me' — 'mostrò' is passato remoto (appeared). The clitic order 'mi si' is typical older/poetic Italian.
segments.paradiso-14-g73: 'che' = 'that.' 'tra quelle vedute' = 'among/amongst those sights/visions.' 'si vuol lasciar' is an impersonal present construction ('it is wished/it is fitting to let/allow'); 'si' makes the phrase impersonal. 'che non seguir la mente' literally 'that the mind not follow' — here Dante uses an infinitive after 'lasciar' instead of a subjunctive clause; the sense is 'it is fitting to let the mind not follow' (i.e., to refrain from attempting to follow or describe further the vision).
segments.paradiso-14-g48: Ma = 'but' (contrast). Sì come is the comparative 'just as' introducing a simile. carbon is an archaic form for 'carbone' (coal/charcoal). che fiamma rende is a relative clause: che = 'that' and rende is present indicative of rendere meaning 'yields/emits' (the coal yields flame). Word order is straightforward: simulacrum 'just as [coal] that yields flame'.
segments.paradiso-14-g49: e = 'and', linking the second circumstance of the simile. per + noun expresses cause/means: per vivo candor = 'by/through a vivid whiteness' (vivo = 'vivid, lively' modifying candor = 'whiteness/brightness'). quella soverchia = 'that surpassing/excessive one' (quella pointing back to the whiteness and soverchia as an attributive adjective used poetically).
segments.paradiso-14-g50: sì che = 'so that' introducing the consequence of the previous clause. la sua parvenza = 'its appearance' (la + possessive sua). si difende = reflexive present indicative of difendere, 'defends itself' or 'its appearance wards itself off' — si is the reflexive pronoun attached to the verb; the verb here states a present, general fact about the appearance.
segments.paradiso-14-g51: così = 'thus/so' closing the simile. questo folgór = 'this lightning' (folgór is an archaic spelling). che già ne cerchia is a relative clause: già = 'already', ne + cerchia = 'encircles (it/us/thence)' (ne is an enclitic pronoun referring back to context), cerchia is present indicative of cerchiare. fia vinto is a future form (fia = future/conditional form used by Dante for 'will be') + past participle vinto = 'will be overcome/ subdued'. in apparenza = 'in appearance' (limiting the sense). da la carne = 'by the flesh' (da + article la, here meaning 'by bodily matter' or 'by the body/sensuality'). che tutto dì la terra ricoperchia is another relative clause: tutto dì = 'all day/continually', ricoperchia is an archaic/poetic form of ricoprire 'covers'. Overall: 'so this lightning ... will be, in appearance, overcome by the flesh that continually covers the earth.'
segments.paradiso-14-g52: né = 'nor/not even' (negative conjunction). potrà is future of potere 'will be able'. tanta luce = 'so great a light' (tanta intensifies luce). affaticarne = infinitive affaticare + enclitic pronoun ne = 'to tire it' (ne refers back to the previously mentioned flesh or body); the verb appears in the infinitive with the enclitic attached, a normal poetic form.
segments.paradiso-14-g53: ché = 'for/because' giving the reason. li organi del corpo = 'the organs of the body' (li is the plural article used in Dante; organi here = bodily organs or the faculties/senses). saran is the future of essere (saranno) 'will be'. forti = 'strong' (able, robust). a tutto ciò che = 'for all that which' (a + tutto ciò che). potrà dilettarne = future potrà + dilettare + enclitic ne = 'will be able to delight it' / 'that can give it delight' — ne again refers back to the body/organism. Future tense throughout expresses certainty about bodily capacity.
segments.paradiso-14-g54: Tanto = 'so, to such an extent.' mi is the dative 'to me' and parver is an archaic/dialectal past-form/poetic form of parere meaning 'they seemed' (verb agrees with an implicit plural subject). sùbiti e accorti are predicates/adjectives describing that subject ('sudden/quick and attentive/aware'). e l’uno e l’altro coro = 'both the one and the other choir' (a paired expression meaning both choirs). a dicer is an infinitival construction 'to say' (a + infinitive used to indicate purpose or immediate action). «Amme!» is an exclamation (Amme = 'to me' or 'ah me' in this context).
segments.paradiso-14-g55: A single punctuation mark (comma) indicating a pause and continuation of the previous thought; no lexical items to analyze here.
segments.paradiso-14-g56: che = 'which' introducing a clause commenting on the previous exclamation; ben = 'clearly/well' (intensifier). mostrar is the infinitive 'to show' used here as part of the construction 'show desire' (Dante often uses infinitives in reportage). disio d’i corpi morti = 'desire for the dead bodies' (d’ = di + article; corpi morti = corpses/bodies that are dead).
segments.paradiso-14-g57: forse = 'perhaps' marking a tentative comment. non pur = 'not only' (non + pur/pure used here to mean 'only'/'merely'). per lor = 'on account of them' / 'for them' (per + pronoun lor = 'for them'). This short clause suggests the desire might not be solely for the dead bodies themselves.
segments.paradiso-14-g58: ma = 'but' (contrast). per le mamme = 'for the mothers' (per + definite article + noun). The phrase adds other reasons/objects of longing beyond the dead bodies themselves.
segments.paradiso-14-g59: per li padri e per li altri = 'for the fathers and for the other ones' (repetition of per + article for emphasis). che fuor cari = 'who were dear' — note fuor is an archaic/dialectal form here (contextually a past-tense or relative marker; Dante uses such contracted forms). anzi che = 'before' in the temporal sense here ('before they became'), fosser is an archaic subjunctive/inflected form of essere (to be) used by Dante (fosser = 'they should be/were to become'), sempiterne fiamme = 'everlasting flames' (i.e., their later eternal state). The whole tail means these desires are for those who were dear before they became eternal flames.
segments.paradiso-14-g60: Ed ecco = 'and behold' (Ed is simply e + d for poetic linking). intorno = 'around' or 'surrounding' — a locative adverb indicating where the following phenomenon appears.
segments.paradiso-14-g61: di chiarezza pari = a prepositional phrase 'of equal clarity/brightness'; di + noun + adjective is a compact attributive phrase describing the quality of what is about to appear (i.e., a lustro of the same brightness). pari = 'equal' modifying chiarezza.
segments.paradiso-14-g62: nascere un lustro = 'a gleam/brightness was born/arose' (nascere used impersonally to mean 'arose'). sopra quel che v’era = 'above what was there' (sopra = above; quel che = what; v’era = vi era = 'was there' in the imperfect tense). The infinitive nascere is used in narration after ecco to report the sudden appearance.
segments.paradiso-14-g63: per guisa d’orizzonte = 'in the manner of a horizon' (per guisa = 'in the fashion/manner of'); che rischiari = relative clause 'that/which brightens/illuminates' — rischiari is a present form meaning 'brighten' or 'make clear/light up'. The clause compares the new lustro to an illuminating horizon.
segments.paradiso-14-g74: Quindi is an adverb meaning 'thereupon/then.' Ripreser is an archaic past form of riprendere ('to resume' or 'recover') and here governs the clause; word order shows an inversion: virtute (the agentive power) is placed after the object phrase li occhi miei, so the sense is roughly 'virtue resumed my eyes.' a + infinitive (rilevarsi) introduces the action 'to raise/raise themselves,' and rilevarsi is reflexive (si) meaning 'to lift oneself' or 'to raise one's eyes.'
segments.paradiso-14-g75: e links the next statement. Vidimi is a past-tense verb with enclitic mi ('I saw myself' or 'I perceived myself'), here introducing a result; translato is a past participle used predicatively ('transferred/transported'). Sol means 'alone' or 'only' and qualifies the company (con mia donna). in più alta salute means 'into a loftier/higher state of salvation' with salute used in a spiritual sense.
segments.paradiso-14-g76: Ben m’accors' io is an inverted, contracted form of mi accorsi (past tense of accorgersi, 'I perceived/realized'); Ben is an intensifier ('well/clearly'). Ch’ introduces a content clause ('that') and io era più levato uses era (imperfect/past) plus levato (past participle/adjectival) to indicate a past state 'I was raised/more elevated.'
segments.paradiso-14-g77: per introduces the cause or means ('by/through the ...'); l’affocato is an adjectival form meaning 'overheated/blazing' or intensely hot, modifying riso ('smile' here, not the noun 'rice'); de is an older form of di ('of'), and la stella is 'the star' (the celestial body causing the effect).
segments.paradiso-14-g78: che begins the relative/comparative clause 'which,' mi is the dative/indirect object with parea (imperfect of parere, 'seemed'), so che mi parea = 'which seemed to me.' più introduces the comparative, roggio is an archaic adjective meaning something like 'more fiery/reddish' (here poetic and not the common modern lexeme), and che l’usato is the comparative 'than the usual,' with l’usato = 'the customary/usual.'
segments.paradiso-14-g79: Con tutto ’l core = 'with all my heart' (con + tutto + definite article + core); e con quella favella links 'and with that speech.' ch’è una in tutti is a relative clause: ch’ = che 'which/that,' è = 'is,' una = 'one' emphasizing unity ('one in all' = a single common speech shared by everyone). Word order is typical of Dante's poetic inversion.
segments.paradiso-14-g80: a Dio feci olocausto is simple past action: a Dio 'to God,' feci is first-person past (feci = I made/did) and olocausto ('holocaust' in the older sense, a burnt offering) is the direct object. The structure means 'I made a burnt offering to God.'
segments.paradiso-14-g81: qual functions as a relative/indefinite 'such as/as was fitting'; conveniesi is an impersonal or literary past form meaning 'was fitting' or 'suited' (convenire 'to be fitting'); a la grazia novella = 'to the new grace' (a + definite article + grazia; novella here qualifies grazia as 'new' or 'recent'). The clause qualifies the kind of offering made.
segments.paradiso-14-g82: Non er’ anco is a negative clause in archaic diction: er’ is a contracted form of era (was) and anco here means 'yet/still', so non er’ anco = 'was not yet'. Del mio petto is a simple genitive phrase ('from/of my breast'), and essausto is a past participle used adjectivally after the noun (literally 'my breast exhausted'), so the whole phrase means 'the ardor ... had not yet been exhausted from my breast.'
segments.paradiso-14-g83: Ch’io conobbi introduces a subordinate content-clause headed by the verb conobbi (passato remoto 'I perceived/realized'). Ess o functions as an anticipatory object pronoun referring back to 'l'ardor'; stato is the past participle ('been') and forms the perfect/passive idea with accetto e fausto as predicative adjectives ('accepted and auspicious'). Note that litare is an odd/archaic word here and does not fit a normal modern pattern (see notes below).
segments.paradiso-14-g84: Ché (= 'for/because') introduces the reason; con tanto lucore e tanto robbi is an instrument/description phrase ('with so much radiance and so much ...'), and m’apparvero is the compound form mi + apparvero (mi enclitic to the verb) in passato remoto, literally 'appeared to me'. The verb precedes its (postposed) subject splendor here (a common poetic inversion), and dentro a due raggi indicates location 'within two rays.'
segments.paradiso-14-g85: Ch’io dissi links the previous perception to the speaker’s utterance: ch’ = 'that/and', io = 'I', and dissi is passato remoto 'I said'. The colon anticipates the following direct speech.
segments.paradiso-14-g86: O Elïòs is a vocative address ('O Helios/Sun'); the diaeresis over ï signals separate syllabification. Che sì li addobbi is a relative/characterizing clause addressing the Sun: che = 'who/which', sì = 'so/thus', li is a clitic object ('them', referring to the lights/rays) placed before the verb, and addobbi is the 2nd-person singular present form (older/Tuscan ending -i) meaning 'you adorn' — so 'O Helios, who so adorns them' or 'O Helios, you who so adorn them.'
segments.paradiso-14-g27: Quell’ uno e due e tre: an emphatic listing (Quell’ = quello elided) naming the three divine Persons. che sempre vive e regna: a relative clause; vive and regna are present indicatives meaning 'lives' and 'reigns', with sempre repeated to stress eternal continuity. in tre e ’n due e ’n uno: the preposition in is repeated to express mutual indwelling (the poetic idea that God is simultaneously in three, in two, and in one); ’n is the contracted form of in.
segments.paradiso-14-g28: non circunscritto: non negates the adjective circunscritto (past participle used adjectivally) — 'not circumscribed/limited'; this is a negative attribute ascribed to the divine subject.
segments.paradiso-14-g29: e tutto circunscrive: e links the previous clause; tutto here functions as a totalizing direct object/adverb ('all' / 'entirely'), and circunscrive is a present indicative verb 'circumscribes' or 'encompasses' — the paradoxical statement that the same God who is not limited nevertheless envelops everything.
segments.paradiso-14-g30: tre volte era cantato: tre volte (three times) is an adverbial phrase; era cantato is the imperfect/passive (or perfect passive in older usage) 'was sung' indicating repeated past action. da ciascuno di quelli spirti: 'by each of those spirits' (da = by as agent). con tal melodia: 'with such a melody' — tal qualifies the manner of singing.
segments.paradiso-14-g31: ch’ad ogne merto saria giusto muno: ch’ (che) introduces the result/consequence clause. ad ogne merto = 'to/for every merit' (ad + ogni), saria is the conditional/future-in-the-past form 'would be' (archaic for sarebbe), giusto = 'just', and muno (from Latin munus) means 'gift/portion/reward' — altogether, 'that to every merit there would be a just recompense.'
segments.paradiso-14-g32: E io udi’ ne la luce più dia del minor cerchio una voce modesta: E io udi’ ('And I heard' — udi' = udii, past historic/perfect). ne la luce più dia del minor cerchio = 'in the light more bright of the lesser circle' (ne = in; più dia is a comparative phrase 'more bright/clear' with dia an archaic adjective), una voce modesta = 'a modest/plain voice' heard within that light. Word order is poetic: prepositional phrase precedes the noun.
segments.paradiso-14-g33: forse qual fu da l’angelo a Maria,: forse = 'perhaps'; qual fu = 'such as was' (qual + fu, fu = past of essere); da l’angelo a Maria = 'from/by the angel to Mary' (da marks the agent for the example). The line compares the modest voice to the angel’s speech to Mary.
segments.paradiso-14-g34: risponder:: an infinitive used here to introduce the reported speech; in Dante this form functions like an historic or narrative verb ('answered:'), and it directly leads into the quoted reply.
segments.paradiso-14-g35: Quanto fia lunga la festa di paradiso,: Quanto = 'how' or 'how long'; fia is the archaic/future subjunctive or future form of essere ('will be' / 'may be'); lunga modifies festa ('feast'), and di paradiso specifies whose feast it is. The clause poses the measure or duration of Paradise's feast.
segments.paradiso-14-g36: tanto il nostro amore si raggerà dintorno cotal vesta.: tanto = 'to the same extent/so much'; il nostro amore is the subject; si raggerà is future middle voice of raggare (here 'will radiate' or 'will beam'), with si marking the reflexive/mediopassive; dintorno = 'around'; cotal vesta = 'such a garment/vesture'. The clause states a proportional relation between the duration of the feast and the outward radiance of their love.
segments.paradiso-14-g37: La sua chiarezza séguita l’ardore;: 'La sua chiarezza' is subject ('its clarity/brightness'); séguita is an archaic verbal form (here meaning 'followed' / 'follows' — a poetic imperfect/present of seguire); l’ardore is the object/point of reference. The line states a sequence: clarity attends or follows ardor.
segments.paradiso-14-g38: l’ardor la visïone,: elliptical clause continuing the sequence: 'the ardor [follows] the vision' — the verb is omitted by ellipsis but understood from surrounding lines; word order is compressed for poetic effect.
segments.paradiso-14-g39: e quella è tanta,: e = and; quella = 'that' (the vision just mentioned); è = is; tanta = 'so great' or 'so much' — a simple copular clause stating the magnitude of the vision.
segments.paradiso-14-g40: quant’ ha di grazia sovra suo valore.: quant’ (quanto) = 'as much as' or 'to the extent that'; ha = 'has' (present indicative); di grazia = 'of grace' (grace as an unmerited gift); sovra suo valore = 'above its own worth' (sovra = above; suo valore = its due worth). The clause qualifies how great the vision is: in proportion to grace exceeding the recipient's merit.
segments.paradiso-14-g41: Come la carne glorïosa e santa fia rivestita,: Come = 'as' or 'just as'; la carne glorïosa e santa = 'the glorious and holy flesh'; fia rivestita = future/passive 'will be clothed' (fia = archaic future of essere; rivestita = past participle used predicatively). The simile begins with the final state of the glorified body.
segments.paradiso-14-g42: la nostra persona più grata fia per esser tutta quanta;: la nostra persona = 'our person'; più grata fia = 'will be more pleasing' (comparative + future); per esser = 'by being' (per + infinitive expresses cause/reason); tutta quanta = 'entirely/whole' (intensive phrase). The clause states the consequence for the person when the flesh is glorified.
segments.paradiso-14-g43: per che s’accrescerà ciò che ne dona di gratüito lume il sommo bene,: per che = 'wherefore/and so'; s’accrescerà = reflexive future 'will be increased' (si + accrescerà); ciò che ne dona = 'that which gives to/for us of it' (ciò che = 'that which'; ne = pronoun referring back to something given/of it/us); di gratüito lume = 'of gratuitous light' (gratüito = unmerited, freely given); il sommo bene = 'the highest Good' (agent of donation). The clause means that the free light bestowed by the supreme Good will be augmented in us.
segments.paradiso-14-g44: lume ch’a lui veder ne condiziona;: lume = 'light' (subject); ch’ a lui veder = relative clause 'which for seeing him' (a + pronoun + infinitive veder = 'to see him'); ne condiziona = 'conditions/influences it' (ne refers back to the seeing/experience). In sense: the light is what enables/conditions our seeing of God.
segments.paradiso-14-g45: onde la visïon crescer convene,: onde = 'therefore/whence' (introduces consequence); la visïon = 'the vision'; crescer convene = 'it is fitting/necessary that it grow' (crescer infinitive + convene impersonal, 'convene' = 'is fitting/necessary'). Word order is inverted for poetic effect.
segments.paradiso-14-g46: crescer l’ardor che di quella s’accende,: crecer (infinitive) precedes the subject l’ardor — 'the ardor must grow' — followed by the relative clause che di quella s’accende ('which is kindled by that/it'); s’accende is reflexive/passive 'is kindled' or 'kindles itself.'
segments.paradiso-14-g47: crescer lo raggio che da esso vene.: same pattern — crescer (infinitive) + lo raggio (subject) = 'the ray must grow'; che da esso vene is the relative clause 'which comes from it' (da = from; vene = comes). The three parallel clauses list what must increase as a consequence.
segments.paradiso-14-g87: This segment contains only a punctuation mark (a single period) and carries no lexical material to analyze; it functions as a pause/stop in the text.
segments.paradiso-14-g88: 'Come' introduces a simile ('as/like'). 'Distinta da minori e maggi lumi' is a past-participle phrase meaning 'separated/distinguished from lesser and greater lights' and grammatically modifies 'Galassia' (which appears later); word order is inverted for poetic effect so the descriptive phrase precedes the noun it qualifies. 'Tra ’ poli del mondo' is a locative phrase 'between the poles of the world', while 'Galassia sì' places the noun 'Galassia' at the end with 'sì' as an intensifier ('so/indeed').
segments.paradiso-14-g89: The clause begins with the relative/conjunctive 'che' ('which/that') and uses 'fa' + infinitive 'dubbiar' to mean 'causes to doubt' (literally 'makes [one] doubt'). 'Ben' is an intensifier ('well/very') modifying 'saggi' ('wise ones/sages'). The verb 'fa' is present and impersonal in the sense 'it causes'.
segments.paradiso-14-g90: 'Sì costellati' is a predicate participle phrase ('so/stars-studded'), describing 'quei raggi' which is the subject that follows later. 'Facean' is the archaic imperfect 3rd-person-plural of 'fare' (modern 'facevano'), 'they made/formed'. In the word order here the subject 'quei raggi' comes after the verb phrase: 'those rays so studded in the deep made Mars the venerable sign...' — 'Marte' functions as the object/predicative noun (they made/formed Mars/the sign). The relative clause 'che fan giunture di quadranti in tondo' ('which make the joinings of quadrants round') modifies 'segno'. Note: 'tondo' here is adjectival 'round' (sense list lacked that option).
segments.paradiso-14-g91: 'Qui' is a locative adverb 'here/at this point'. 'Vince' is present indicative 3rd-singular 'overcomes/surpasses'. The object 'la memoria mia' (my memory) precedes the verb by inversion, and 'lo 'ngegno' (the ingenuity/intellect) is the subject; note the archaic elision '’ngegno' for 'ingegno' after the article 'lo'.
segments.paradiso-14-g92: 'Ché' = 'for/because'. 'Quella croce' is the subject and 'lampeggiava' is the imperfect tense 'was flashing' or 'flashed repeatedly/appeared in flashes'. The word order places the object/predicate 'Cristo' after the verb, so the construction reads 'that cross flashed Christ' (i.e., Christ appeared/was revealed on that cross).
segments.paradiso-14-g93: 'Sì ch’io' = 'so that I' (sì + che, with contracting apostrophe). 'Non so trovare' is present 'I do not know how to find' (non + sapere + infinitive). 'Essempro' is an archaic spelling of 'esempio' ('example') and 'degno' means 'fitting' or 'worthy'; together 'I cannot find a fitting example.'
segments.paradiso-14-g94: 'Ma' = 'but'. 'Chi' is the indefinite relative 'whoever' (not a specific person). 'Prende' and 'segue' are present indicative verbs 'takes' and 'follows' respectively, forming a proverbial clause: 'whoever takes up his cross and follows Christ.' 'Sua croce' is reflexive/possessive 'his own cross.'
segments.paradiso-14-g95: 'Ancor' (archaic) = 'still/yet'. 'Mi scuserà' is future 3rd-singular 'will excuse me' (scusare). 'Di quel ch’io lasso' literally 'for that which I, weary' — 'ch’io lasso' is an elliptical descriptive clause where 'lasso' ('weary') qualifies the poet; the meaning is 'will still excuse me for that (failure/omission) because I am weary.'
segments.paradiso-14-g96: 'Vedendo' is a present participle ('seeing'); it heads a participial clause 'seeing Christ flash in that dawn.' 'In quell' albor' = 'in that dawn' (aphetic apostrophe after the article). 'Balenar' is the infinitive 'to flash' used after the participle; 'Cristo' is the object/predicate of the flashing ('Christ flash').
segments.paradiso-14-g97: Di corno in corno and tra la cima e ’l basso are spatial expressions ('from horn to horn' and 'between the top and the low part'). Si movien lumi uses the reflexive/impersonal si with movien (archaic 3rd-plural form of muovere/muover) and lumi (lights) as the grammatical subject: 'lights moved' or 'lights were moving.'
segments.paradiso-14-g98: Scintillando forte is a present participle phrase ('sparkling/gleaming strongly') modifying the lights; nel congiugnersi insieme e nel trapasso uses nel + gerund-infinitive nouns to mark the two circumstances in which they shine: 'in joining together' and 'in the passing/passage.'
segments.paradiso-14-g99: Così si veggion qui diritte e torte: così ('thus/so') links back to the previous action; si veggion is an impersonal/passive reflexive present-tense observation ('are seen' / 'one sees'); qui = 'here'; diritte e torte are predicates describing the visible forms ('straight and bent/crooked').
segments.paradiso-14-g100: Veloci e tarde are two coordinate adjectives ('swift and slow/late') modifying the things being seen or moving; the simple juxtaposition gives a contrast of tempo.
segments.paradiso-14-g101: Rinovando vista is a participial phrase: rinovando (renewing/repeating) modifies vista ('sight' / 'the view'), so 'renewing sight' or 'renewing the view' — it indicates that the seeing is being refreshed as the lights move.
segments.paradiso-14-g102: Le minuzie d’i corpi: le minuzie ('the small details') is the subject, and d’i corpi is a genitive ('of the bodies'); Dante uses the elided form d’i (= di + i) typical of the period.
segments.paradiso-14-g103: Lunghe e corte are coordinate adjectives ('long and short') describing the 'minuzie' just mentioned; the word order places the contrasting adjectives after the noun-group they qualify (understood from context).
segments.paradiso-14-g104: Moversi is the infinitive or reflexive infinitive 'to move themselves' (here used to describe what the details do); per lo raggio is 'along/by means of the ray', onde = 'from which/whereby', si lista (si + list a) means 'is edged/striped' (listare = to edge/stripe), and talvolta l’ombra che introduces the shadow which is sometimes so edged — the clause ties the motion to the ray that shapes the shadow.
segments.paradiso-14-g105: Per sua difesa is a short prepositional phrase: per = 'for' or 'for the sake of', sua = 'its', difesa = 'defense/protection' — here it explains the purpose/motive for the following action.
segments.paradiso-14-g106: La gente con ingegno e arte acquista: la gente is the agent ('people'), con ingegno e arte ('with ingenuity and skill') are instrumental phrases, and acquista is a 3rd-person singular present (or present-habitual) meaning 'acquire/gain' — so 'people acquire (it) by craft and skill.'
segments.paradiso-14-g1: A simple prepositional path expressed by two contracted prepositions: "Dal" = da + il (from the) and "al" = a + il (to the). The phrase sets up the direction of motion (from the center to the circle).
segments.paradiso-14-g2: Coordinated clause: "e sì" (= and likewise/so) links the reciprocal motion. "Movesi" is a reflexive present form (verb before the subject here): literal "moves itself"; the subject "l'acqua" follows and is marked by the article. "in un ritondo vaso" is a locative/instrumental phrase describing the container in which it moves.
segments.paradiso-14-g3: "Secondo" here means "according to/depending on." "Ch’ è percosso" uses the contraction "ch'" = che (that) plus essere + past participle percosso, i.e. "is struck"; "fuori o dentro" specify whether the striking is outside or inside.
segments.paradiso-14-g4: "Ne la mia mente fé sùbito caso" is an older idiom: "fe" (archaic past of fare) + "caso" = an occurrence was made/occurred in my mind. Word order is inverted: the thing that occurred ("questo ch'io dico") comes after the verb; "ne" means "in/into" here.
segments.paradiso-14-g5: "Sì come" introduces a simile (as/just as). "Si tacque" is an archaic past tense (tacque = fell silent) used impersonally/reflexively: "became silent." The phrase modifies the previous clause by comparison.
segments.paradiso-14-g6: "Per la similitudine che nacque" — "per" = because/by means of; "nacque" (simple past of nascere) = "arose/originated." The phrase identifies the cause: a simile born of his speech and that of Beatrice ("del suo parlare e di quel di Beatrice").
segments.paradiso-14-g7: "A cui" is an indirect-object phrase (to whom). "Sì cominciar" is an infinitive shortened for cominciare; here the infinitive anticipates the construction with "piacque" (it pleased). "Sì" modifies manner (thus/so).
segments.paradiso-14-g8: A simple adverbial phrase: "dopo lui" = "after him."
segments.paradiso-14-g9: "Piacque" is the past tense of piacere (impersonal: 'it pleased'). It completes the construction started by the preceding infinitive phrase: 'to whom it pleased to begin thus.'
segments.paradiso-14-g10: "A costui" = to this man (indirect object). "Fa mestieri" is an idiom: "fa mestieri" = "is necessary/it is needed" (literally 'does need'); the verb "fa" is used impersonally with the person marked by the prepositional phrase.
segments.paradiso-14-g11: "E nol vi dice" contains the contraction "nol" = non + lo (not it/him), with "vi" as a dative/indirect object (to you). The clause means 'and he does not tell it to you.' The coordinate negatives "né ... né" (nor ... nor) enumerate manners: neither with the voice nor even in thought.
segments.paradiso-14-g12: A nominal infinitive clause functioning as the object: "d'un altro vero andare a la radice" = 'of going to the root of another truth.' "D'" = di (of), "andare" is the infinitive 'to go/proceed', and "radice" = root/origin.
segments.paradiso-14-g13: "Diteli" is an imperative (di + te + li contracted) meaning 'tell them.' "Se" introduces an indirect question 'whether.' "La luce onde s'infiora vostra sustanza" is a relative clause: "onde" = from which; "s'infiora" is reflexive present (is adorned/bloomed) used passively of the substance.
segments.paradiso-14-g14: "Rimarrà" is future indicative ('will remain'). "Con voi" = with you. "Sì com' ell' è ora" is a comparative formula: "sì come" = just as; "ell'" is an archaic clitic for 'it/she' referring back to the light; "ora" = now.
segments.paradiso-14-g15: "E se rimane" = 'and if it remains'; "se" is the conditional/if particle and "rimane" is present indicative ('remains'). This sets up the conditional request that follows.
segments.paradiso-14-g16: "Dite" is an imperative plural ('say/tell'); "come" = 'how.' The speaker commands that, if the light remains, they should explain in what manner.
segments.paradiso-14-g17: "Poi che" = 'after that/once' (poetic sequence marker). "Sarete" is future of essere ('you will be'); "visibili rifatti" is a past-participle adjective phrase ('made visible again' / 'restored to visible form').
segments.paradiso-14-g18: "Esser porà" is an archaic impersonal construction meaning roughly 'it may be/it will be possible' (infinitive + a future/impersonal particle). "Ch' al veder non vi nòi" is a subordinate clause: "ch'" = that; "al veder" = 'at seeing' or 'in the act of seeing'; "vi" = to you; "nòi" is an archaic verb-form meaning 'to become weary/annoyed' (here 'not grow weary').
segments.paradiso-14-g19: "Come" is the comparative conjunction 'as/just as' introducing the simile that follows.
segments.paradiso-14-g20: "Da più letizia pinti e tratti" uses past participles as adjectives: "pinti" (painted/colored) and "tratti" (drawn/stroked) governed by "da più letizia" = 'by greater joy' (agent/cause). The phrase describes how they are adorned by increased joy.
segments.paradiso-14-g21: "A la fïata" = at each breath/occasion (fïata = 'time/moment'); "quei che vanno a rota" = those who go round the wheel. The verbs "levan la voce e rallegrano li atti" have normal S-V order (subject 'quei' with verbs 'levan' and 'rallegrano') and describe raising the voice and brightening gestures.
segments.paradiso-14-g22: "Così" = so/thus; it signals that what follows is parallel to the previous imagery.
segments.paradiso-14-g23: "A l'orazion pronta e divota" is a prepositional phrase ('at the ready and devout prayer'). "Pronta" and "divota" are adjectival qualifiers describing the prayer that prompts the circles' rejoicing.
segments.paradiso-14-g24: "Li santi cerchi mostrar nova gioia" — subject "li santi cerchi" (the holy circles) performs the verb "mostrar" (show). "Nel torneare e ne la mira nota" specifies where/when: in their turning and in the admirable/remarked note. Several nouns are medieval or used poetically; some supplied sense options do not match the intended meaning (see senses).
segments.paradiso-14-g25: "Qual si lamenta" = 'whoever laments/one who complains.' "Perché qui si moia per viver colà sù" is a purpose/cause clause: "per" + infinitive expresses 'in order to live up there.' Note: "si moia" is an older/subjunctival-style reflexive form used here of 'morire' (to die); the provided lemma in the word list does not match that archaic sense (see senses).
segments.paradiso-14-g26: "Non vide quive lo refrigerio de l'etterna ploia" — "non vide" is simple past negative ('did not see'). "Quive" is an archaic demonstrative/adverb meaning 'here/there in this place.' The phrase states that such a complainer has not experienced the refreshing effect of the eternal rain here.
segments.paradiso-14-g107: E + come introduces a simile: 'And as' begins the comparison. The two nouns 'giga e arpa' are simply coordinated instruments (fiddle and harp); word order is straightforward and poetic (no article). 'E' at the start links this simile to what went before.
segments.paradiso-14-g108: The prepositional phrase 'in tempra tesa di molte corde' describes the state of the instruments: 'in tempra' (in tuning/attunement) modified by the adjective 'tesa' (stretched/taut). 'di molte corde' specifies the strings — literally 'with many strings' — with the preposition 'di' linking the noun 'tempra' to its specification.
segments.paradiso-14-g109: 'Fa' is third-person singular present ('makes'); 'dolce tintinno' is the direct object ('a sweet tinkling'). The phrase 'a tal da cui...' marks the indirect beneficiary: 'to such a one to whom...' — here 'da' + 'cui' is an archaic/poetic way to form 'to whom' or 'for whom'. 'La nota non è intesa' uses the past participle 'intesa' in a passive sense: 'the note is not perceived/heard/understood.'
segments.paradiso-14-g110: 'Così' links this clause to the preceding simile ('so/thus'). 'Da’ lumi' is a contraction of 'da i lumi' = 'from the lights'; the relative clause 'che lì m’apparinno' ('that there appeared to me') modifies 'lumi.' The reflexive clitic 's’' on 'accogliea' marks a reflexive/passive sense ('was received/gathered'); 'accogliea' is imperfect, giving an ongoing or repeated past action. 'Per la croce' is an instrumental/mediating phrase ('by/through the cross'), and 'una melode che mi rapiva' is a relative clause: 'a melody that enraptured/transported me.'
segments.paradiso-14-g111: 'Sanza' is the archaic form of 'senza' ('without') and takes the infinitive that follows. 'Intender l’inno' is an infinitive clause 'to understand/perceive the hymn' — together they mean 'without understanding the hymn.' The order 'sanza intender l’inno' is a compact, poetic infinitival complement.
segments.paradiso-14-g112: 'Ben' (well/clearly) emphasizes perception. The archaic 'm’accors’' is a contracted past-form meaning 'I noticed/I became aware' (equivalent to modern 'mi accorsi'); the pronoun 'io' is emphatic. 'Ch’elli era d’alte lode' is a subordinate clause: 'that it/they was/were of high praise' — here 'd'alte lode' means 'of lofty praise.' Note: some lemmas here are archaic and their dictionary senses in the supplied list do not exactly match the usage, so the verb is best understood as a past-perceptive construction.
segments.paradiso-14-g113: 'Però ch’' here functions causally ('for/because') introducing the reason. 'A me venìa' uses the imperfect ('was coming/was being brought to me') describing words or exclamations reaching Dante; the quoted words 'Resurgi' and 'Vinci' are being heard as utterances (imperatives). The comparative clause 'come a colui che non intende e ode' ('as to one who neither understands nor hears') uses two present indicative verbs in parallel.
segments.paradiso-14-g114: The emphatic subject 'Ïo' (I) plus the reflexive imperfect 'm’innamorava' ('I was falling/was in love') indicates a continuing or habitual state in the past. 'Tanto' marks degree ('so much'), and 'quinci' (archaic) indicates place or starting-point ('from here/at that point').
segments.paradiso-14-g115: This result clause begins with 'che' ('that'). '’Nfino a lì' (contracted 'in fino a lì') means 'up to there/until then.' 'Non fu alcuna cosa' is a perfect simple negative ('there was no thing'); the relative clause 'che mi legasse' uses the imperfect subjunctive 'legasse' after a negative/indefinite antecedent ('alcuna cosa') to express a non-existent or potential binding. 'Con sì dolci vinci' ('with such sweet bonds') uses 'sì' as an intensifier and 'vinci' (here 'bonds/ties') as the object of 'legasse.'
segments.paradiso-14-g116: 'Forse' ('perhaps') hedges the statement. 'La mia parola' is the subject; 'par' (archaic 'pare') is present third-person singular 'seems.' 'Troppo osa' is an adjectival phrase: 'troppo' (too) modifies the adjective 'osa' (daring); 'osa' agrees with 'parola' (feminine) and here functions as 'bold/daring.'
segments.paradiso-14-g117: 'Posponendo' is a gerund ('putting aside' / 'postponing') and governs the noun phrase 'il piacer de li occhi belli' ('the pleasure of the beautiful eyes'). 'De li' is the archaic form of 'dei' ('of the'). The whole phrase indicates that the speaker is temporarily setting that pleasure aside in what follows.
segments.paradiso-14-g118: 'Ne’ quai' is a contraction of 'ne i quali' meaning 'in whom' or 'in whom of whom'; 'mirando' (present gerund/participle) ('by gazing') expresses the manner, and the clause 'mio disio ha posa' ('my desire finds repose') uses 'ha posa' ('has a resting-place') — present indicative showing the effect produced by looking.
segments.paradiso-14-g119: 'Ma chi s’avvede' ('but whoever notices') introduces a generalizing concessive: 'whoever perceives that...' 'I vivi suggelli d’ogne bellezza' means 'the living seals of every beauty' (an image: living signs or confirmations of beauty). The comparative-correlation 'più fanno più suso' literally 'make more, more upward' means 'they raise it ever higher' — 'più...più' is the standard Italian correlative comparative.
segments.paradiso-14-g120: 'E ch’io non m’era lì rivolto a quelli' is a further clause: 'and that I was not there turned toward them.' 'Non m'era rivolto' uses the imperfect indicative to describe a past state ('I was not turned/turned my face'); 'rivolto' is a past participle used adjectivally ('turned toward').
segments.paradiso-14-g121: 'Escusar puommi' is an archaic modal construction: 'puommi' (from 'può a me') means roughly 'may it be to me' / 'I may be excused.' The speaker says one may excuse him 'di quel ch’io m’accuso' ('for that which I accuse myself of'), 'per escusarmi' ('in order to excuse myself' / 'by excusing myself'). The infinitives and clitics reflect older, idiomatic placement.
segments.paradiso-14-g122: 'E vedermi dir vero' uses the construction 'vedermi' + infinitive to mean 'see me [to] say' — idiomatically 'see that I speak truly' or 'observe me speaking the truth.' 'Dir vero' is an infinitive phrase 'to say true' = 'to speak truthfully.'
segments.paradiso-14-g123: 'Ché' ('for/because') introduces the reason: 'the holy pleasure is not here disclosed.' 'L piacer santo' is the subject and 'non è qui dischiuso' uses the past participle 'dischiuso' (literally 'opened') in the passive sense 'is not revealed/disclosed here.'
segments.paradiso-14-g124: 'Perché si fa,' begins the explanation-cause: 'because it becomes/is made...' The impersonal/semi-reflexive 'si fa' means 'it becomes' or 'it is made' and introduces the following result.
segments.paradiso-14-g125: 'Montando' is a gerund from 'montare' ('ascending/rising') indicating the manner or process — 'as one ascends' or 'in mounting.' It links temporally/mannerly to 'si fa più sincero.'
segments.paradiso-14-g126: The comparative predicate 'più sincero' ('more sincere') completes the clause: 'it becomes more sincere.' 'Più' is the comparative adverb and 'sincero' is the adjective describing the resulting state.

sentences.paradiso-14-s13: And just as, at the rising of early evening, nine apparitions begin to appear across the sky, so that the sight seems and yet seems not real, it seemed to me there that new existences began to be seen and to make a circuit outside the other two circumferences.
sentences.paradiso-14-s14: O true scintillation of the Holy Spirit!
sentences.paradiso-14-s15: As it became suddenly bright and blazing to my eyes, which, overcome, could not bear it!
sentences.paradiso-14-s16: But Beatrice appeared to me so beautiful and smiling that, amid those sights, it is fitting to leave the mind not to follow.
sentences.paradiso-14-s9: But just as a coal that yields flame, and by its vivid whiteness — so abundant that its appearance protects itself — so too this lightning that already encircles us will, in appearance, be overcome by the flesh that all day covers the earth; nor will so great a light be able to tire it, for the bodily organs will be strong for everything that can delight it.
sentences.paradiso-14-s10: They seemed to me so quick and alert, and both choirs, the one and the other, to say "Amme!"
sentences.paradiso-14-s11: , which clearly showed desire for the dead bodies: perhaps not only for them, but for the mothers, for the fathers and for those others who were dear before they became everlasting flames.
sentences.paradiso-14-s12: And behold around, of equal brightness, a gleam arose above what was there, like a horizon that lights up.
sentences.paradiso-14-s17: Thereupon my eyes recovered the power to lift themselves; and I saw myself transported, alone with my lady, into a higher state of salvation.
sentences.paradiso-14-s18: I clearly perceived that I had been raised higher by the star’s burning smile, which seemed to me redder than usual.
sentences.paradiso-14-s19: With all my heart and with that one speech common to all, I made to God a burnt offering such as befits the new grace.
sentences.paradiso-14-s20: And the ardor for the sacrifice had not yet been exhausted from my breast, which I perceived to have been accepted and auspicious; for with so much radiance and so many things shining, splendor appeared to me within two rays, and I said, “O Helios, who so adorns them!”
sentences.paradiso-14-s5: That One, Two and Three who ever lives and ever reigns — not circumscribed, and yet circumscribing all — was sung three times by each of those spirits with such a melody, that to every merit there would be a just reward.
sentences.paradiso-14-s6: And I heard, in the brighter light of the lesser circle, a modest voice—perhaps such as the angel to Mary—answer: 'However long the feast of Paradise may be, so will our love radiate around such a vesture.'
sentences.paradiso-14-s7: Its clarity follows the ardor; the ardor follows the vision, and that is so great as the grace it has beyond its own worth.
sentences.paradiso-14-s8: As the glorious and holy flesh will be clothed, our person will be more pleasing for being wholly so; therefore what the supreme Good bestows on us of gratuitous light will be increased — a light that conditions our seeing him; wherefore the vision must grow, the ardor that is kindled by it must grow, the ray that comes from it must grow.
sentences.paradiso-14-s22: As, separated from lesser and greater lights, the Galaxy gleams between the poles of the world, so that it makes even the very wise doubt;
sentences.paradiso-14-s23: Here my memory yields to my intellect; for that cross flashed Christ, so that I cannot find a fitting example; but whoever takes up his cross and follows Christ will yet excuse me for that in which I, weary, (fail), seeing Christ flash in that dawn.
sentences.paradiso-14-s24: From horn to horn, and between the summit and the base, lights moved, sparkling strongly in joining together and in the passing; thus here are seen straight and twisted, swift and slow, renewing sight, the minute details of the bodies, long and short, moving along the ray by which the shadow is sometimes edged off, which people by craft and skill acquire for its protection.
sentences.paradiso-14-s1: From the center to the circle, and likewise from the circle to the center the water moves in a round vessel, according as it is struck on the outside or the inside: into my mind this that I say immediately came up, just as the glorious life of Thomas fell silent, because of the simile that arose from his speech and that of Beatrice, to whom it so pleased, after him, to begin: "This man needs it, and he does not tell you — neither with his voice nor even in thought — of going to the root of another truth."
sentences.paradiso-14-s2: Tell them whether the light from which your substance is adorned will remain with you eternally as it is now; and if it remains, say how, after you have been made visible again, it can be that you will not grow weary at seeing.
sentences.paradiso-14-s3: Just as, painted and traced by greater joy, those who go round the wheel at each breath raise their voices and brighten their gestures, so, at the ready and devout prayer, the holy circles showed new delight in their turning and in the admirable note.
sentences.paradiso-14-s4: He who laments that one dies here in order to live up there has not seen here the refreshment of the eternal rain.
sentences.paradiso-14-s25: And just as fiddle and harp, with many strings tightly tuned, make a sweet tinkling to one for whom the note is not perceived, so from the lights that appeared to me there was gathered, by means of the cross, a melody that carried me away, without understanding the hymn.
sentences.paradiso-14-s26: I clearly perceived that it was of lofty praise; for 'Resurgi' and 'Vinci' came to me as to one who neither understands nor hears.
sentences.paradiso-14-s27: I was so much in love there that, up to that time, there had been nothing that bound me with such sweet bonds.
sentences.paradiso-14-s28: Perhaps my words seem too bold, putting aside the pleasure of those beautiful eyes in which, by looking, my desire finds repose; but whoever perceives that the living seals of every beauty raise it ever higher, and that I was not there turned toward them, may excuse me for what I accuse myself of in excusing myself, and may see me speak truly: for the holy pleasure is not disclosed here, because, as one mounts, it becomes more sincere.
